title: Auto-lock accounts after N failed logins (negative pack login monitoring)
business_reason: Detection alone isn't enough — an account under sustained brute force should be locked automatically. The login scan now suspends an active, non-super-admin account once it crosses the failed-login lock threshold.

design.lock: runSuspiciousLoginScan: for each brute-force identity with >= 10 failures, resolve the staff by (unique) email; if active AND not super_admin, set staff_status=suspended + write a staff_access_events record (event_code auto_locked, event_source system) + audit. Returns auto_locked count.
design.safety: super admins are skipped (and the last-super-admin precondition also protects them); only active accounts are locked; threshold (10) is above the alert threshold (5).

param_14.question: What happens to an account under sustained brute force?
param_14.answer: Once an identity crosses the failed-login lock threshold (10), the login scan automatically suspends the matching active, non-super-admin account, records an auto_locked access event, and audits it. Super admins are never auto-locked. It runs on-demand and in the daily sweep.
param_14.security_implications: active mitigation (not just detection); super-admin-safe; auditable; idempotent (a suspended account is skipped on re-scan).
param_14.approval_implications: an auto-locked account requires a governed reactivation.
param_14.downstream_dependencies: per-IP lockout; notify-on-lock; auto-unlock after cooldown.

verify: npx playwright test auto_lock suspicious_login login_anomaly
